title: Portfolio Optimization Agent
description: Scores and ranks the SKU launch shortlist and generates an evidence-based demand range. Two decisions: which SKUs/channels to launch, and the expected demand range + initial confidence interval.
tooling: MILP · weighted scoring · like-product regression · Monte Carlo
transformation_note: Brand-team picks become AI scores across like-products performance and shelf velocity, which the planner approves. (Demand-range decision is ENABLED·AUTOMATED.)

title: Market Segmentation Agent
description: Clusters channels/customers and ranks launch timing & sequencing by launch readiness and demand potential.
transformation_note: Marketing-calendar dates give way to channels and customers ranked by launch readiness and demand potential.

title: NPI Forecast Agent
description: Generates a live multi-level (brand → SKU → customer) demand curve and flags model drift.
tooling: Statistical models (ARIMA, time series) · ML (XGBoost, prophet) · like-product analogy
transformation_note: One static 12-week baseline becomes a live multi-level sales curve that captures product life cycle sales forecast.

title: Signal Capture Agent
description: Ingests and weights POS, retailer orders, and digital/unstructured signals in near real time; harmonizes & validates the demand dataset. Shared across steps 2 and 7.
tooling: Data engineering tools · GenAI for unstructured signal extraction
transformation_note: POS, retailer orders and digital signals are ingested and weighted in near real time, not pulled weekly by hand.

title: Demand Sensing
description: Aera skill (deterministic). Reads demand and builds the sell-through curves that show what is happening at the shelf — mapping point-of-sale scans up to cases — and continuously recalibrates the in-market forecast curve. Shared across steps 2 and 7. In the agentic vision this is wrapped as a CustomAgent worker.
transformation_note: The sales curve is recalibrated automatically from detected demand signals — running continuously, not recomputed by hand on demand.

title: Demand Signal Reconciliation
metric: One aligned demand number across systems
description: Aera skill (deterministic). Reconciles the PlanX, Kinaxis and Commercial demand signals into a single aligned demand view so operations plan against one agreed number instead of three competing ones.
tooling: Cross-system signal reconciliation · alignment rules (PlanX · Kinaxis · Commercial)
transformation_note: PlanX, Kinaxis and commercial demand signals are reconciled into one aligned demand view automatically, instead of being cross-checked by hand.

title: Inventory Optimization and Planning Agent
description: Computes the optimal launch quantity from the underage/overage trade-off (newsvendor one-time-buy) and publishes it as the target for downstream planning systems.
tooling: Newsvendor type of one-time buy model
transformation_note: Launch quantity becomes an optimized recommendation balancing stockout and write-off cost for planner sign-off. (Publish-to-downstream decision is ENABLED·AUTOMATED.)

title: Inventory deployment and replenishing agent
description: Sizes pre-build, positions stock across DCs/regions, allocates to customers/channels with risk buffers, and triggers replenishment within the approved envelope.
tooling: Multi-echelon inventory optimization and replenishment models
transformation_note: A static pre-build plan becomes an agent recommendation sized to demand confidence intervals for planner sign-off. (Positioning is TRANSFORMED·CONTINUOUS; channel allocation is ENABLED·AUTOMATED.)

title: Lifecycle Management Agent
description: Updates the demand curve via phase-in/phase-out lifecycle models and flags required supply/inventory/distribution actions for the planner.
tooling: LLM · statistical & ML lifecycle forecast models (phase-in / phase-out)
transformation_note: The demand curve updates and required supply and distribution actions are flagged for the planner to act on.

title: Exception Handler Agent
description: Scores and routes exceptions (shortages, delays, demand spikes) by severity and generates a proposed next-best-action resolution.
tooling: LLM · exception alerting tools · next-best-action generation
transformation_note: Exceptions are scored and routed by severity with a proposed resolution, instead of manual firefighting.

title: Performance Monitoring Agent
description: Tracks KPIs (MAPE, WMAPE, Bias), performs root-cause analysis, and auto-flags model degradation vs. plan.
tooling: KPI metrics · accuracy & bias formulas (MAPE, WMAPE, Bias)
transformation_note: MAPE, sell-through and margin are tracked against plan automatically, with model degradation auto-flagged.

title: Scaling Agent
description: Identifies expansion opportunities, simulates scaling scenarios, and triggers replenishment in high-velocity markets for planner approval.
tooling: Scenario simulator · forecasting models on simulated parameters
transformation_note: Expansion opportunities are identified and replenishment triggered in high-velocity markets for planner approval.

title: Learning Loop Agent
description: Writes NPI actuals back into the like-product library and forecast models, closing the loop each cycle. Backed by Distiller MemTree persistent memory.
tooling: ML · feedback loop models · like-product library updates
transformation_note: NPI actuals are written back into the like-product library and forecast models, closing the loop each cycle.

title: On-Shelf Availability
description: Aera skill (deterministic). Reaches into distributor and customer shelf and warehouse data to see what is on-shelf and in the warehouse; when service levels slip, pushes out corrective actions. Running today — fills this step's shelf-availability gap ahead of the future agentic version.
tooling: Retailer/distributor data connectors · service-level rules · push alerts
transformation_note: Shelf and warehouse positions are read continuously and service-level gaps trigger corrective pushes, instead of manual account-by-account checks.

title: Supply Chain Loss Prevention
description: Aera skill (deterministic). Prevents supply-chain loss by cutting production during the frozen period when demand is not manifesting — protecting against over-build and write-off. Drives supply planning and inventory pre-builds in Kinaxis. Running today within this otherwise-placeholder step.
tooling: Frozen-period plan vs. live demand · production-cut decision logic
transformation_note: Frozen-period production is cut automatically when demand fails to manifest — no manual per-cycle review, instead of building to a stale plan.

title: Touchpoint 1 · Launch Plan Approval
description: Review agent-generated launch scenarios and confidence bands; approve initial supply, inventory, and distribution plan.

title: Touchpoint 2 · Exception Review
description: Review the scored and ranked NPI exception queue; decide accept agent recommendation or override with judgment.

title: Touchpoint 3 · Post-Launch Recalibration
description: Review sell-through vs. forecast; approve scaling or wind-down; confirm updated supply plan for the next launch window.

title: NPIControlTowerOrchestrator
description: Planner-as-Launch-Control-Tower router over the 8-step NPI super-agents; governs the 25+ → 3 touchpoints surface. Memory (MemTree): persists approved decisions, sell-through outcomes, and like-product precedents across NPI cycles.

title: Aera
description: Called decision-intelligence service — returns forecast, inventory & optimization answers on request · Skills · Cortex. Invoked as a node inside pipelines; NOT an orchestrator.
